terms.definition: A straight path that connects two points. It has a fixed length with a clear beginning and end.
terms.exampleUsage: The edge of your eraser is a line segment.

terms.definition: A straight path that starts at one point and goes on forever in one direction. It has one endpoint.
terms.exampleUsage: A beam of sunlight coming through a window is like a ray.

terms.definition: The amount of turn between two lines or rays that meet at a common point called the vertex.
terms.exampleUsage: When you open a book halfway, the two covers form an angle.

terms.definition: An angle that measures exactly 90 degrees, like the corner of a square or rectangle.
terms.exampleUsage: The corners of your notebook are all right angles.

terms.definition: Two lines that are always the same distance apart and never cross each other, no matter how long they are.
terms.exampleUsage: The two rails of a railway track are parallel lines.

terms.definition: Two lines that cross each other at a right angle (90 degrees), forming an L shape or a plus sign.
terms.exampleUsage: The floor and the wall of your room meet at perpendicular lines.

terms.definition: A closed shape made of 3 straight sides and 3 angles. The angles always add up to 180 degrees.
terms.exampleUsage: A slice of pizza is shaped like a triangle.

terms.definition: A triangle where all three sides are the same length and all three angles are equal (60 degrees each).
terms.exampleUsage: A yield road sign is shaped like an equilateral triangle.

terms.definition: Any closed shape with exactly 4 straight sides and 4 angles. The word 'quad' means four.
terms.exampleUsage: A kite, a square, and a rectangle are all quadrilaterals.

terms.definition: A four-sided shape with 4 right angles. Opposite sides are equal in length and parallel.
terms.exampleUsage: A door is usually shaped like a rectangle.

terms.definition: The point where two sides of a shape meet to form an angle. The plural is vertices.
terms.exampleUsage: A triangle has 3 vertices, one at each corner.

terms.definition: A four-sided shape where all four sides are equal in length, like a tilted square.
terms.exampleUsage: The diamond shape on a playing card looks like a rhombus.
